objections.description: Artifact is truncated mid-component tree at the Library tab panel. Sections 5-8 (Design Token System, Core Layout Shell, Key Screens, Motion System, Dark/Light Theme Switching) are completely missing. This is Round 5 and the spec is incomplete.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide complete artifact with all sections. The deliverables list requires: design token system, core layout shell, at least 3 key screens, motion system, dark/light theme switching. None of these are present in this truncated artifact.

objections.description: Spec relies heavily on the `inert` attribute for accessibility (modal focus trapping, tab panel visibility). Browser support floor is Firefox 90+, but inert is only supported from Firefox 97+. Safari 15.0 doesn't support inert (added in 15.1). This creates a gap for the declared support matrix.
objections.suggested_fix: Either: (1) Add a polyfill like `wicg-inert` to package.json and load it in main.jsx before any UI renders, or (2) Revise browser support floor to Safari 15.1+ and Firefox 97+, or (3) Document the fallback behavior when inert is unavailable (aria-hidden + tabindex=-1 on all focusable descendants).

objections.description: Component architecture mentions 'React.memo — see memo pattern below' multiple times but the memo comparator implementation is never shown. Without explicit comparators, React.memo uses shallow equality which often fails for objects/arrays passed as props, negating the performance benefit.
objections.suggested_fix: Define the memo comparator pattern explicitly. Example for TimelineItem: React.memo(TimelineItem, (prev, next) => prev.id === next.id && prev.status === next.status && prev.timestamp === next.timestamp). Or use a generic deep comparator utility for objects.

objections.description: CI step 'parses meta.json, fails build if dist/main.js gzipped > 80KB' is described but scripts/check-bundle-size.js implementation is not provided. The budget breakdown estimates CSS at ~4KB but CSS is loaded separately — this conflates JS budget with total transfer size.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide the check-bundle-size.js implementation. It should use esbuild's metafile format, calculate gzip size using zlib, and exit with non-zero code if exceeded. Separate JS budget (80KB) from total transfer budget in documentation.

objections.description: Banned APIs list (AbortController.timeout, Array.at, Object.hasOwn, structuredClone, crypto.randomUUID) is documented but there's no automated enforcement. Developers will accidentally use these.
objections.suggested_fix: Add eslint-plugin-escompat or configure ESLint with custom rule to ban these APIs. Alternatively, add a babel plugin that transforms them to polyfills. Document which approach is chosen.

objections.description: Scroll restoration uses an in-memory LRU Map but doesn't specify the implementation. Rolling your own LRU has edge cases (capacity boundary behavior, delete vs pop).
objections.suggested_fix: Either use a proven LRU library (tiny-lru is ~1KB) or provide the exact implementation in utils.js. The spec should show the code, not just describe it.

objections.description: icons/ directory has .jsx files but also an index.js barrel file. Mixed extensions (.jsx vs .js) is inconsistent. esbuild handles both, but this affects editor tooling and grep-ability.
objections.suggested_fix: Standardize: either all icon files are .jsx (since they contain JSX), or rename the barrel to index.jsx for consistency.

suggestions.description: Add a Step M0: Create a git branch named 'frontend-react-migration' before any changes. This allows atomic PR review and easy git revert --no-commit to compare before/after.

Document the exact terminal commands for dev workflow: 'Terminal 1: cd src/frontend/web && npm run dev' and 'Terminal 2: python src/backend/apps/web_server.py'. New developers shouldn't have to infer this.

The 150ms debounce is good, but also consider: (1) Don't announce if the new route is the same as current (hash changes without actual navigation), (2) Clear the live region content after announcement to prevent re-reading on screen reader focus.
